memoryLane.name: Phase 6: Docker Infrastructure Audit & Workflow Implementation
memoryLane.description: Complete infrastructure audit findings, implement memory-lane lifecycle, clean up stale scripts, fix agent frontmatter. Full Docker MCP integration with selective rehydration for token efficiency.

tasks.title: Confirm & Execute Script Deletions (Option C Priority 1)
tasks.description: Delete 7 stale scripts (tmp-timeline-*, crop-*, clear-cache-*, spin-test). Review 3 conditional scripts (mcp-platinum-validate, validate-wave2, validate-build-*). Move example code to docs/examples/. Clean up config/memory-lanes-animation.json (unused). Post-cleanup target: 14 scripts (44% reduction from 25).
acceptance_criteria: 7 stale scripts deleted from /scripts
3 conditional scripts reviewed and decision made (keep/delete)
2 example scripts moved to docs/examples/
Unused config file archived or deleted
Git commit: 'chore(scripts): Remove stale Docker and phase-specific scripts'
Verify: pnpm run docker:mcp:smoke passes (Docker integration still healthy)

tasks.title: Implement Selective Rehydration (Option C Priority 2)
tasks.description: Build memory-lane-aware rehydration to load only active lane entities (2-3) instead of all entities (50+). Token savings: 470 tokens/session, 9,400 tokens/month. Create scripts/load-active-memory-lane.mjs that detects git branch, reads config, calls open_nodes([selective entities]). Update SessionStart hook to use selective load.
tasks.audit_reference: Config & Memory-Lane Lifecycle Audit - Selective Rehydration Mechanism

implementation_outline.logic: 1. Detect git branch 2. Load config/memory-lanes/{branch-lane}.json 3. Extract docker_entities to load 4. Call mcp__MCP_DOCKER__open_nodes([selective entities only])

acceptance_criteria: Script load-active-memory-lane.mjs created and tested
SessionStart hook updated to use selective load
Config file structure validated (phase-6-infrastructure-and-workflows.json exists)
Test: orchestrator-bootstrap loads selective entities in <5 seconds, ~30 tokens
Git commit: 'feat(memory): Implement selective rehydration for memory-lane efficiency'
Documentation: README.md updated with token savings metrics

tasks.title: Build Memory-Lane Lifecycle CLI (Option C Priority 3)
tasks.description: Create scripts/lane-lifecycle.mjs with commands: open, sync, close, list. Automate memory-lane state transitions (creation, checkpoint updates, archival). Wire to package.json as npm lane:* commands. Implement config validation (sync checks between Docker and config).
tasks.audit_reference: Config & Memory-Lane Lifecycle Audit - Hook Integration & Automation
acceptance_criteria: lane-lifecycle.mjs fully functional with 4 commands
validate-memory-lanes.mjs checks config/Docker sync
npm lane:open creates config file + Docker entity + git branch
npm lane:sync updates config + Docker observations
npm lane:close archives config + marks entity completed
npm lane:list shows active and archived lanes
All commands tested with phase-6 lane
Git commit: 'feat(lanes): Add lane-lifecycle CLI for memory management'

tasks.title: Fix 5 Agent Frontmatter Fields (Option B)
tasks.description: Add missing 'role' and 'trigger' fields to 5 agents: code-generation, knowledge-memory, mcp-automation, planning, skill-builder. Standardize delegation gate pattern. Validate frontmatter compliance against .claude/rules/frontmatter-schema.md. Add optional 'return-format' field for consistency.
tasks.audit_reference: Skills & Agents Quality Audit - Frontmatter Compliance Status

template.role: [One or two sentence description of responsibility]

acceptance_criteria: All 5 agents have 'role' field (2 sentences max)
All 5 agents have 'trigger' field (clear invocation conditions)
All 5 agents have 'return-format' field
Frontmatter validated against .claude/rules/frontmatter-schema.md
No duplicates or conflicts with existing SME agents
Git commit: 'fix(agents): Add missing frontmatter fields to 5 agents'

blockers.title: Docker tools availability clarification
blockers.description: System message lists mcp__MCP_DOCKER__ tools as available, but they were not accessible in function definitions. Need confirmation that tools are properly loaded for next session.
blockers.workaround: Tasks structured as if tools are available; will resolve in fresh session with full context
